mnemonics.title: Navigation Component Parts
mnemonics.content: N-A-V-H-A-C: NavGraph, Action, Argument, Host (NavHostFragment), Controller (NavController), DeepLink

mnemonics.title: Intent Types
mnemonics.content: E-I: Explicit Intent = Immediate/Inside app; Implicit Intent = Intent/Investigate system handlers

mnemonics.title: Navigation Patterns
mnemonics.content: D-B-T-N: Drawer, Bottom, Tab, Nested - four main patterns in Android

acronyms.usage: Used in deep linking to define the address pattern that maps to app destinations
Type-safe argument passing between navigation destinations to prevent runtime crashes
The structural design of shared information environments including navigation systems
XML resource file containing all destinations and actions in the navigation system

visualTips.visualization: Think of it as a subway map - stations are destinations (Fragments), lines are actions connecting them, and transfers are navigation events
Imagine a stack of plates - pop removes the top plate (fragment), push adds a new one. The bottom plate is the starting destination.
URI is like a postal address - it tells the system exactly which 'house' (destination) to deliver the user to in the app
Activity is the house (only one main structure), Fragments are rooms inside - you navigate between rooms without leaving the house

analogies.analogy: NavController is like a GPS system - it knows all the routes (navigation graph), tracks where you've been (back stack), and guides you to new destinations.
Intent filters are like job requirements - they specify what kind of task (intent) a component (activity/fragment) can handle, and the system matches the right candidate.
Navigation Drawer is like a restaurant menu - hidden until requested, but contains all the main options (navigation choices) available to the user.
Deep linking is like a direct flight - instead of going through connections (home screen), it takes users straight to their specific destination.
